architecture.title: Architecture & Technology

items: Microsoft Azure cloud platform
Modern .NET (6–10) and ASP.NET Core
REST APIs and distributed systems
Entity Framework Core
Microservices architecture
High availability and fault tolerance
CI/CD and DevOps practices
Security and data protection
.NET MAUI apps for Android and iOS

aiHighlight.label: Built in, not bolted on
aiHighlight.title: Artificial intelligence across the stack
aiHighlight.text: AI is a first-class part of our architecture, not an add-on. Agents keep external reference data current on their own, documents and photographs are read into structured fields, incoming messages are parsed into operational records, and entire products are localized into dozens of languages automatically. Every model call returns schema-validated data, is cached against its inputs, and stays traceable to its source.

about.title: About Aegis AO Soft
about.subtitle: Secure, reliable software systems for regulated and mission-critical environments

intro.description: Aegis AO Soft develops secure, reliable software systems for organizations operating in regulated and mission-critical environments. The company specializes in platforms that support operational continuity, regulatory compliance, and large-scale data processing.
intro.leadership: Founded and led by a veteran software architect, Aegis AO Soft combines hands-on technical leadership with decades of experience delivering production systems for government-adjacent industries and complex operational domains.
intro.ai: Artificial intelligence is a working part of that engineering rather than a layer added on top. It localizes our platforms into dozens of languages, keeps external reference data current without anyone rechecking it by hand, and reads the documents and messages that our clients' operations actually arrive as — under the same reliability requirements as every other part of the system.

missionCritical.title: Mission-Critical Operational Systems
missionCritical.description: Our work focuses on systems that must function continuously and accurately, often under regulatory oversight or operational constraints.
missionCritical.design: Solutions are designed for reliability, auditability, and long-term maintainability.

technology.philosophy: Technology choices prioritize long-term supportability over short-term trends.

leadership.experience: The founder brings decades of experience designing and implementing complex systems in regulated environments, including:
leadership.approach: This approach ensures architectural consistency, technical accountability, and rapid resolution of complex issues.

commitment.title: Commitment to Reliability and Accountability
commitment.description: Aegis AO Soft engages with organizations that require dependable systems and a long-term technology partner. Our objective is to deliver solutions that remain stable, maintainable, and auditable throughout their operational lifecycle.

ai.title: Artificial Intelligence in Our Products
ai.description: We treat AI as an engineering component, not a feature to advertise. It is used where a task genuinely requires judgment over unstructured input — language, documents, messages, or sources that change without notice — and deterministic code is used everywhere else.

localization.title: Localization and Translation at Scale
localization.text: Reaching a new market should not mean rewriting an application by hand.
items: Aegis Localizer scans a codebase, separates user-facing text from machine strings, translates it and rewrites the source to read from resource files — across .NET, web, Android, Apple and Flutter projects.

Marketplace product descriptions written by a seller in one language are translated automatically into every language the storefront serves, including indigenous languages with almost no translation market.
Rental storefronts run in 15 languages, and this website itself is published in 9, produced through the same pipeline.
Translations are validated before they ship: a rendering that loses or invents a formatting placeholder is rejected rather than published.

agents.title: Autonomous Agents That Maintain Data
agents.text: Some data goes stale on someone else's schedule. Agents keep it current instead of a person rechecking it by hand.
items: Toll rates change by authority decision, without announcement. Research agents read the toll authorities' own published sources, detect changes and update pricing across the United States and Canada.
Agents work through the obstacles a human researcher would: navigating portals, handling sources that publish rates only as interactive calculators rather than tables.
Findings are recorded with their source, so every rate can be traced back to where it came from and reviewed before it takes effect.
The same pattern applies wherever an external reference dataset has to stay accurate without a person watching it.

documents.title: Documents and Images
documents.text: Operational systems receive information as photographs and scans, not as clean data.
items: Driver's licences are captured by phone camera and read into structured fields for verification.
Machine-readable barcodes are decoded first and treated as authoritative; visual recognition fills only what the barcode does not carry.
Results are cross-checked between sources rather than trusted from a single reading.
The same approach extends to invoices, statements and other operational paperwork that arrives as an image.

messages.title: Unstructured Messages and Events
messages.text: Integrations are often one-way: a partner platform sends email, not an API call.
items: Booking notifications from partner rental platforms are parsed into structured reservations — dates, vehicle, driver, identifiers.
Templates change without warning. When a message no longer matches the expected shape, an AI pass recovers the meaning instead of the integration silently failing.
Deterministic parsing runs first and AI only handles what it could not resolve, which keeps both cost and unpredictability down.
Anything still ambiguous is flagged for a person rather than guessed at.

principles.title: How we keep it dependable:
items: Models return schema-validated structured data, never free-form prose the system has to interpret.
Cheap deterministic filters run before any model call, so the expensive step only sees what actually needs judgment.
Results are cached and tracked against their inputs: unchanged work is never paid for twice, and changed input is detected and redone.
Every AI-derived value keeps a link to its source and remains reviewable by a person.
When confidence is low the system reports instead of guessing — a missing value is recoverable, a confidently wrong one is not.

ai.philosophy: AI earns its place in a product by removing work that would otherwise be done by hand, under the same reliability requirements as the rest of the system.

vehicleRental.subtitle: Launch Your Own Branded Rental Site in Minutes

Every tenant is resolved by hostname — a free subdomain or your own custom domain — with isolated fleet, pricing, branding, and customer data, all configurable from the owner portal without a single line of code.

adminGuide.sectionSubtitle: Complete guide for managing your Mercadinho25 marketplace
adminGuide.recommendationTip: For the most accurate automatic translations, enter product names and descriptions in Portuguese or English.

gettingStarted.title: 1. Getting Started
gettingStarted.intro: Mercadinho25 is a multi-tenant e-commerce marketplace platform designed for Brazilian artisan goods. This guide covers all administrative functions available in the Dashboard.

login.title: Logging In
steps: Navigate to your store URL (e.g., yourstore.mercadinho25.com)
Click "Login" in the top navigation bar
Enter your email and password
After login, click your profile avatar and select "Dashboard"
login.tip: Default Admin Credentials: admin@mercadinho.com.br | Password set during initial setup

roles.title: User Roles
roles.intro: The platform uses role-based access control (RBAC) with four levels:
roles.seller: Seller — Create/manage products, view sales, manage own orders
roles.tenantAdmin: TenantAdmin — Manage sellers, users, settings, categories, payments
roles.superAdmin: SuperAdmin — Multi-tenant management, create tenants, system-wide access

dashboard.title: Dashboard Overview
dashboard.intro: The main dashboard displays key metrics at a glance:
items: Total Products: Number of active products in your store
Total Orders: All orders with pending order count
Revenue: Total sales revenue with currency formatting
Active Sellers: Number of approved sellers in your marketplace

categories.title: Managing Categories
categories.intro: Categories organize your product catalog. Navigate to Dashboard > Products > Categories.
steps: Click "Add Category" to create a new category
Enter the category name in your current language
Check "Auto-translate" to automatically translate the name to all 10 languages (including indigenous languages)
Set the category slug (URL-friendly name, auto-generated)
Optionally set a parent category for subcategories (hierarchical structure)
Toggle "Active" to control visibility
Click "Save" to create the category

translations.title: Category Translations
translations.intro: Each category name is stored in all 10 platform languages (including indigenous languages):
items: Auto-translate generates translations via Google Translate or Claude AI
The API returns localized names based on the user's interface language
You can manually override any auto-translated name
Missing translations fall back to Portuguese, then to the first available

attributes.title: Adding Attribute Types to a Category
attributes.intro: Attribute types define variant options for products (e.g., Color, Size, Material):
steps: Open the category edit modal
Scroll to "Attribute Types" section
Click "Add Attribute Type" — select from existing or create new
When creating new: enter names in all 10 languages (with country/culture flags), slug auto-generates
Configure Display Order (hierarchy) and Required toggle
Click the gear icon to manage values (e.g., Red, Blue, Green for Color)
Each value supports multilingual names and optional HEX color codes
Removing an attribute type deletes all related variant data — use with caution
Click "Save" to apply
attributes.tip: The display order matters: the first attribute type becomes the top-level selector, the second becomes the next level. Values cascade — lower levels filter based on higher-level selections.

creating.title: Creating a Product
steps: Click "Add Product"
Fill in Name, Description, Story (multilingual fields)
Set pricing: Base price, sale price (optional), currency
Upload images (drag-and-drop or file picker)
Select category and subcategory
Configure variants with individual pricing/stock
Set stock quantity and SKU
Set status: Draft, Active, or Archived
Click "Save" to publish

phoneUpload.title: Uploading Images from Phone (QR Code)
phoneUpload.intro: Upload product photos directly from your phone camera:
steps: Click "Upload from Phone" button (smartphone icon)
On desktop: scan the QR code with your phone camera
On mobile: the app redirects to the camera capture page automatically
Take photos — they upload in real-time to the product form
A green indicator shows "Waiting for photos..." while active
Click "Done — Add Images" when finished
phoneUpload.tip: The QR session is time-limited. Both devices must be on the same network. You can regenerate the QR code if it expires.

autoTranslate.title: Automatic Translation
autoTranslate.intro: The platform translates product names and descriptions to all supported languages:
steps: Fill in Name and Description in Portuguese or English (recommended for best accuracy)
Check the "Auto-translate" checkbox (sparkle icon ✨)
On save, the system translates to all 7 other languages automatically
Uses Google Translate API or Claude AI (configured by admin)
Review and manually edit any translation after saving
autoTranslate.tip: Speech-to-text is also available: click the microphone icon next to any text field to dictate in the selected language.

variants.title: Adding Variants to a Product
variants.intro: When the selected category has attribute types, the variant section appears automatically:
steps: Select a category — attribute types load automatically (e.g., Color, Size)
Enable each attribute type by checking its checkbox (strict hierarchical order)
Check specific values (e.g., Red, Blue under Color; S, M, L under Size)
Click a value to highlight it (green border). All highlighted = editing specific variant
Price panel switches: "Base Prices" ↔ "Variant Prices: Color: Red, Size: Large"
Set Price, Compare-at Price, Stock for each combination
Variants without custom prices inherit the base product price
Unchecking a value removes all related variant data automatically
variants.tip: Color attributes show swatches. Variant names auto-generate in all languages (e.g., "Red / Large"). A blue info panel shows whether you're editing base or variant prices.

bulkOps.title: Bulk Operations & Search
items: Search by product name or SKU
Filter by status (Draft, Active, Archived) and category
Bulk share to Facebook, Instagram, or Etsy
Bulk status change (activate, archive, draft)

shipments.title: Shipments
items: View all shipments with status tracking
Create shipping labels (Correios integration for Brazil)
Track packages with real-time status updates
Print labels directly from the dashboard
Statuses: Pending, Label Created, Posted, In Transit, Out for Delivery, Delivered, Returned, Lost, Cancelled

reports.title: Reports & Analytics
items: Sales Analytics: Revenue charts, order trends, top-selling products
Orders Tab: Complete history with filtering and pagination
Export data for external analysis
View metrics by date range

tenants.title: Tenant Management (SuperAdmin Only)
tenants.intro: Navigate to Dashboard > Store > Tenancy to manage multiple stores.
steps: Click "Add Tenant" to create a new marketplace
Configure: Store name, subdomain, custom domain (optional)
Set commission rate (default 10%)
Assign a Tenant Admin user
Activate/deactivate tenants as needed
tenants.tip: Subdomains and custom domains are configured automatically when creating a tenant. All DNS records are provisioned without manual intervention.

sellers.title: Seller Management
items: View all sellers with stats (products, sales, rating)
Approve or reject pending seller applications
Edit seller profiles (store name, description, location)
Toggle "Featured" status for prominent display
View Stripe Connect onboarding status
Filter by status: Pending, Approved, Suspended

users.title: User Management
items: View all registered users with their current roles
Change user roles (Customer, Seller, TenantAdmin)
Only SuperAdmin can assign SuperAdmin role
Search and filter by name, email, or role

stripe.title: Stripe Configuration
stripe.sandbox: Sandbox: Enter test API keys (pk_test_..., sk_test_...) → Save → Test → Activate
stripe.production: Production: Same steps with live keys. Test thoroughly before going live. Configure webhook endpoint.
stripe.connect: Stripe Connect: Each seller completes onboarding to receive payments. The platform auto-splits payments based on commission rate.

mercadopago.title: MercadoPago
items: Enable/disable MercadoPago payments
Configure marketplace fee percentage
Set default currency (BRL)
Enable Checkout Pro for seamless flow
Supports: Credit/Debit cards, PIX, Boleto

shipping.title: Shipping (Correios)
items: Configure Correios integration for Brazilian shipping
Set shipping rates and delivery options
Enable/disable methods per region
Configure free shipping thresholds

google.title: Google Shopping
steps: Copy the generated product feed URL
Create a Google Merchant Center account
Add the feed URL in Merchant Center > Products > Feeds
Configure settings (country, language, currency)
Submit for review

etsy.title: Etsy Integration
items: Connect your Etsy shop via OAuth
Sync products with automatic listing creation
Manage Etsy orders from the dashboard
View analytics and sales data
Bulk publish products to Etsy

social.title: Social Media
items: Facebook & Instagram: Connect Business page, share products, configure Meta product feed
WhatsApp Business: Configure number per seller, enable contact button on product pages
Configure feed languages and translation priorities

branding.title: 6. Branding & Customization
items: Logo: Upload store logo (header and emails)
Favicon: Custom browser tab icon
Colors: Primary (#2E7D32), Secondary (#FFC107), Accent
Typography: Custom font family
Banner Image: Hero image for homepage
Tagline & About Text
Custom CSS for advanced styling

languages.title: 7. Language Settings
languages.intro: The platform supports 10 languages: Portuguese (primary), English, Spanish, French, German, Italian, Japanese, Russian, plus indigenous languages Guaraní Mbyá and Nheengatu for cultural heritage preservation.
languages.tip: Always fill in at least Portuguese and English translations for all products and categories to maximize reach. The system falls back to Portuguese if a translation is missing.

whatIs.title: What is MyEZToll?
whatIs.p1: MyEZToll is a specialized, single-organization platform (NOT multi-tenant) built exclusively for car rental companies and fleet operators who need comprehensive toll, GPS, and violation management.
whatIs.p2: Unlike generic solutions, MyEZToll provides dedicated infrastructure for individual fleet operators with complete control over their toll processing and violation workflows across all 50 U.S. states and Canada.
whatIs.p3: The platform works from both directions: it collects charges from toll authorities and violation issuers, and it independently computes tolls from GPS vehicle traces — so a trip is priced the moment the vehicle passes the gantry, long before the agency invoice arrives.

automatedToll.title: Automated Toll Processing

parkingViolations.title: Parking Violation Management

gpsTolling.title: GPS-Based Tolling
gpsTolling.text: Vehicle GPS traces are matched against a database of real toll booths and gantries, then priced per booking — tolls are known from the trip itself, not only from the agency invoice.

towingAlerts.text: Instant SMS and email alerts for towing, ignition on/off, and trip end — routed to the owner, the active renter, or both.

thirdPartyGps.text: Pluggable telematics integrations — Fleet77 and others — bring vehicles and positions from external trackers alongside our own devices, under one fleet view.

aiRateResearch.text: AI agents autonomously research toll authority sources, discover rate changes, and keep the platform's toll pricing current without manual data entry.

whatIs.title: What is MyEZWheels?
whatIs.p1: MyEZWheels is a multi-tenant storefront platform that lets a vehicle rental company launch a complete, professional booking website without building one. Every tenant is resolved by hostname, so each company runs on its own address with its own fleet, pricing, branding, and customer data — fully isolated from every other tenant on the platform.
whatIs.p2: Start on a free subdomain (yourcompany.myezwheels.com) and move to your own custom domain whenever you are ready. Logos, colors, imagery, page content, and location contact details are all configurable from the owner portal — no redeploy, no developer involved.

hero.title: AI Localization for Any Codebase
hero.subtitle: Point it at a codebase and a language. It finds the hardcoded UI strings, translates them with Claude, and rewrites your source to use resource files.

whatIs.title: What Aegis Localizer Is
whatIs.p1: Every application starts with strings written straight into the code. Making that application speak a second language usually means a developer reading every file by hand, deciding which strings a user actually sees, moving them into resource files, and then rewriting the call sites. It is slow, mechanical work that nobody wants to do twice.
whatIs.p2: Aegis Localizer does that pass for you. It parses your source, separates user-facing text from machine strings such as URLs, SQL and identifiers, asks Claude to classify the ambiguous cases, translates what remains into the languages you asked for, writes the resource files in the format your stack expects, and, when you tell it to, rewrites the source so the code reads from those resources.
whatIs.p3: It is built to be run repeatedly. Verdicts and translations are cached, so a second run costs nothing where nothing changed; a reworded English string goes stale and is redone, while a new language reuses the classification work already paid for.

items.label: Product teams going multilingual:
items.text: an existing single-language application that now needs to ship in five, ten or twenty markets.

items.label: .NET teams:
items.text: C# is parsed with Roslyn, so verbatim, raw and interpolated strings are decoded correctly with exact source spans.

items.label: Web, mobile and cross-platform teams:
items.text: React, Vue, Svelte, Android, iOS and Flutter projects are handled with the same pipeline.

items.label: Platform and build engineers:
items.text: scan-only mode with JSON output turns the tool into a CI gate that catches new hardcoded strings at review time.

items.label: Agencies and consultancies:
items.text: localize a client codebase in an afternoon instead of quoting a multi-week manual project.

pipeline.title: How It Works
pipeline.intro: Six stages, each one designed to spend as little of your API budget as possible and to refuse rather than guess when it is not certain.

scan.title: Accurate Scanning
scan.text: C# goes through Roslyn for exact source spans across verbatim, raw and interpolated strings. Other stacks use tolerant lexers that mask comments and existing lookups while preserving byte offsets, so edits land in the right place.

filter.title: Offline Noise Filter
filter.text: URLs, file paths, GUIDs, MIME types, SQL, date patterns, SCREAMING_SNAKE constants and structural context such as nameof, switch patterns, route attributes and Android ids are discarded locally. Nothing filtered here is ever sent to the API, so you never pay for it.

classify.title: Claude Classification
classify.text: Whatever survives the filter is classified by Claude through a forced tool call, so the answer comes back as schema-validated JSON with a user-facing verdict, a reason and a resource key. The pass is language-independent and cached.

resources.title: Resource Bundles
resources.text: Translations are written into the format your stack expects and merged into what is already there. Existing hand-edited translations are preserved, never overwritten.

rewrite.title: Source Rewriting
rewrite.text: With the apply flag, call sites are rewritten to resource lookups. Every touched file is backed up byte-for-byte first, and any file changed since the scan is skipped rather than corrupted.

incremental.title: Incremental Runs
incremental.text: The tool remembers which English text each translation was made from. Reword a string and it goes stale and is retranslated; change nothing and the run costs nothing at all.

stacks.title: Supported Stacks
stacks.intro: Five platforms, each with its own reader, resource format and rewrite style. Automatic platform detection inspects the project tree, and a working sample project ships for every stack.
stacks.outro: Point the tool at a repository with automatic detection and it works out which of these applies before it reads a single string.

safety.title: Safety and Correctness
safety.intro: A missed string costs you a minute. A broken build costs you an afternoon. The tool is deliberately built to report rather than guess whenever a rewrite is not provably correct.

items.label: Byte-for-byte backups:
items.text: every file the tool rewrites is copied first, so any run can be undone completely.

items.label: Span verification:
items.text: a file edited between the scan and the rewrite is skipped, not patched at stale offsets.

items.label: Placeholder validation:
items.text: a translation that loses or invents a placeholder such as {0}, %s or {{count}} is rejected before it reaches a resource file.

items.label: Absent beats wrong:
items.text: a failed translation leaves the key missing so the application falls back to English, and the next run retries it.

items.label: Merge, not overwrite:
items.text: resource stores merge into existing bundles, so translations a human corrected by hand stay corrected.

items.label: Schema-validated model output:
items.text: classification goes through a forced tool call, so the pipeline consumes structured data rather than parsing prose.

items.label: The manual extraction pass:
items.text: no more reading every file to decide which strings a user actually sees.

items.label: Cost of AI translation:
items.text: an offline filter and a cached, language-independent classification pass mean the API is only asked about strings that might matter.

items.label: Adding the tenth language:
items.text: classification is paid for once; each new language only pays for the translation itself.

items.label: Drift after a copy change:
items.text: translations are tracked against the English they came from, so reworded text is detected and redone.

items.label: Regression on new code:
items.text: scan-only mode with JSON output can fail a build when someone adds a fresh hardcoded string.

items.label: Broken formatting in translations:
items.text: placeholder validation catches the class of bug that only shows up at runtime in a language nobody on the team reads.

cli.title: Command Line Tool
cli.text: Installs as a .NET global tool and runs as a single command against a project directory, with scan-only and apply modes.

gui.title: Local Web GUI
gui.text: A ui command starts a local web interface on a free port and opens the browser, for people who would rather not think about flags.

binaries.title: Self-Contained Binaries
binaries.text: Standalone builds for Windows x64, macOS on Apple silicon and Intel, and Linux x64, with no runtime to install first.

ci.title: CI Integration
ci.text: JSON output plus scan-only mode makes a build gate that reports every new hardcoded string without touching the working tree.

byok.title: Bring Your Own Key
byok.text: The tool talks to the Anthropic API with your own key, so translation spend is visible and controlled on your own account.

hosted.title: Hosted Mode
hosted.text: The same web application can run as a hosted service: it accepts an uploaded archive into a per-request sandbox, refuses server paths, and never spends the operator's credit.

limits.title: What It Does Not Do
limits.intro: Being explicit about the boundaries is part of the design. The tool is meant to remove the mechanical work, not to take responsibility for your release.
items: It does not replace a human reviewer on customer-facing copy, brand voice or legal text.
It does not restructure your application's localization architecture; it fits the resource conventions your stack already uses.
It does not touch strings it cannot rewrite safely; those are reported for you to handle.
It does not run your build or your tests, so review the diff and run them as usual.
It does not do layout work: text length changes between languages are still yours to design for.

core.title: Core
core.text: C# on .NET 9, built as a cross-platform console application with a shared core library.

parsing.title: Parsing
parsing.text: Roslyn (Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.CSharp) for C#, plus purpose-built tolerant lexers for TypeScript, JavaScript, Vue, Svelte, Kotlin, Java, Swift, Objective-C, Dart, XML and HTML.

ai.title: AI
ai.text: Anthropic Claude over the Messages API, with forced tool calls for schema-validated classification, batched requests and bounded concurrency.

web.title: GUI
web.text: ASP.NET Core minimal APIs serving a dependency-free HTML, CSS and JavaScript front end with no build step.

formats.title: Resource Formats
formats.text: RESX, i18next JSON, Android strings.xml, Apple .strings and Flutter ARB, each with its own merging store.

testing.title: Testing
testing.text: xUnit suite that runs fully offline against a fake model, so the pipeline can be verified without spending a token.

platforms.title: Runs On
platforms.text: Windows, macOS and Linux, as a .NET global tool or a self-contained binary.

privacy.title: Data Handling
privacy.text: Only candidate strings are sent to the model; filtered machine strings and the rest of your source never leave the machine.

cta.title: Localize Your Application
cta.text: Whether it is one product that needs to ship in a new market or a portfolio of applications stuck in a single language, we can run the first pass with you and show you the diff before anything is applied.
cta.licenseNote: Aegis Localizer is proprietary software. The source is published so that it can be read and reviewed; it is not open source, and commercial use requires a licence from Aegis AO Soft LLC.

hero.title: The Windows Control Centre for Your Philips Hue Lights
hero.subtitle: A native desktop app for managing lights, rooms, scenes, sensors and switches, talking straight to your bridge on the local network.

whatIs.title: What HueControl Is
whatIs.p1: Philips Hue has apps for phones, tablets and voice assistants. It has never had a proper Windows desktop application. If you sit at a PC all day, controlling the lights in the room you are sitting in means picking up your phone.
whatIs.p2: HueControl fills that gap. It is a native Windows application that connects directly to your Hue bridge over the local network and gives you everything in one window: every light, room, zone and scene, plus the sensors, plugs and switches that the simpler desktop tools ignore.
whatIs.p3: It goes past on and off. You can add and remove devices, rename them, create and edit rooms, zones and scenes, program what a dimmer switch does, check accessory battery levels, and manage several bridges grouped into homes with instant switching between them.

items.label: Windows users with Hue lights:
items.text: anyone who works at a desk and would rather change the lighting from the keyboard than from a phone.

items.label: People with large installations:
items.text: multiple rooms, zones, sensors and switches across more than one bridge, all in a single window.

items.label: Privacy-minded households:
items.text: the application talks only to the bridge on your own network; there is no cloud account and no telemetry.

items.label: Home automation enthusiasts:
items.text: device management and switch programming that the official mobile app makes fiddly or hides.

items.label: Developers:
items.text: a clean, dependency-free WPF and MVVM codebase, published under GPL-3.0 and open to read and modify.

lights.title: Lights and Rooms
lights.text: Per-light and per-room on/off with synchronised brightness sliders. Everything is grouped by room and by device type, so a large installation stays readable.

color.title: Colour and Temperature
color.text: A colour picker with proper conversion between the picker, the Hue colour space and colour temperature, so what you choose on screen is what the lamp produces.

devices.title: Device Management
devices.text: Search for and add new lights, plugs, motion sensors, dimmer switches and buttons; rename them; remove the ones you no longer own. Full management, not just control.

switches.title: Switch Programming
switches.text: Bind a dimmer switch to a room so its ON button recalls that room's scene, and see accessory battery level and last activity at a glance.

scenes.title: Scenes and Zones
scenes.text: Create, rename and delete rooms, zones and scenes, with built-in Hue presets such as Relax, Concentrate, Energize and Savanna Sunset, shown with rendered colour previews.

bridges.title: Multiple Bridges
bridges.text: Group several bridges into homes and switch between them instantly. Discovery runs through the Hue discovery service and falls back to manual IP entry when the network blocks it.

local.title: Local Network Only
local.text: The app speaks directly to the bridge REST API on your LAN. Bridge details are stored in your own Windows profile and nothing is sent anywhere else.

languages.title: Ten Languages
languages.text: The interface ships in ten languages, including Arabic and Hebrew with full right-to-left layout, and switches language live without a restart.

themes.title: Dark and Light Themes
themes.text: A custom borderless window with swappable dark and light palettes, so the app fits whichever way you run Windows.

items.label: No official Windows app:
items.text: the gap this product exists to fill; Hue ships mobile and voice, not desktop.

items.label: Reaching for the phone:
items.text: changing the lights while working should not require a second device.

items.label: Control-only desktop tools:
items.text: most third-party utilities toggle lights; this one adds, removes, renames and configures them.

items.label: Switch configuration:
items.text: programming what a dimmer button does is buried in the mobile app and straightforward here.

items.label: More than one bridge:
items.text: large or multi-building installations are handled as homes with instant switching.

items.label: Cloud dependence:
items.text: the lights are on your network, so the control app should work on your network too.

privacy.title: Privacy and Data
privacy.intro: The design principle is simple: your lighting is a local system, so the software that controls it should behave like one.
items: All communication goes to the Hue bridge on your local network over its REST API.
No account, no sign-in and no cloud service operated by us.
Bridge connection details are stored locally in your Windows user profile.
No analytics, tracking or telemetry of any kind is collected.
The Hue discovery service is contacted only to locate a bridge, and manual IP entry is available if you would rather it were not.
The full source is public under GPL-3.0, so every one of these claims can be checked.

languages.title: Languages and Accessibility
languages.intro: The interface is fully translated, not partially localized, and the layout adapts to the language rather than assuming left-to-right text.
items: English, Spanish, Portuguese, French, German and Russian.
Arabic and Hebrew with full right-to-left layout flipping.
Simplified and Traditional Chinese.
Language switches live, with no application restart.
Every interface string is translated, not just the main screens.

platform.title: Platform
platform.text: C# on .NET 9, targeting Windows 10 and 11 as a desktop application.

ui.title: User Interface
ui.text: WPF with a custom borderless window chrome and swappable dark and light theme resource dictionaries.

architecture.title: Architecture
architecture.text: Hand-rolled MVVM: observable objects, sync and async relay commands, debouncing for slider input, and grouped collections.

bridge.title: Bridge Integration
bridge.text: Philips Hue local bridge REST API, including HTTPS bridges that present the Hue Bridge root certificate on the LAN.

dependencies.title: Dependencies
dependencies.text: Zero third-party NuGet packages. The application is built entirely on the .NET base class library and WPF.

i18n.title: Localization
i18n.text: A custom string catalogue covering the whole interface in ten languages, with flow direction flipping for right-to-left locales.

testing.title: Testing
testing.text: xUnit suite with a stubbed HTTP handler for offline runs, plus opt-in tests that exercise a real bridge.

license.title: Licence
license.text: Free and open source under GPL-3.0, with the full source available on GitHub.

requirements.title: Requirements
items: Windows 10 or Windows 11.
A Philips Hue bridge on the same local network.
The .NET 9 Desktop Runtime, or a self-contained build that bundles it.
Physical access to the bridge button once, to authorise the app on first connection.

cta.title: Try HueControl
cta.text: The application is complete and open source. Read the code, build it yourself, or get in touch if you would like a packaged build or a change made for your installation.
cta.trademarkNote: HueControl is an independent application and is not affiliated with, endorsed by, or sponsored by Signify N.V. or Philips. "Philips Hue" is a trademark of Signify Holding.
